$comment: c47 (D-CSUBSET-FNPTR-ARRAY-SIZE-INFERENCE): a `[]` (inferred-size) array of FUNCTION POINTERS with an initializer infers its length from the brace count — the c34 inference, but the `[]` lives on the INNER declarator inside the parenthesized group `(*const NAME[])(sig)`, folded by the declarator engine's group-recursion arm. c34 relaxed only the top-level array suffix; the group arm hardcoded allowFlexibleArray=false (the c10 FAM gate), so an inferred fn-ptr-array `[]` wrongly fired S_NonConstantArrayLength (S000B). c47 threads an init-inference signal (allowInitInferredArray) that crosses into the group while FAM still does NOT (c10 preserved), so the inner `[]` becomes incomplete and is sized once from the brace count (Pass 1.5). Frontier: sqlite3.c:186647 `static int (*const sqlite3BuiltinExtensions[])(sqlite3*) = { sqlite3Fts5Init, … };` (mirrored as a LOCAL by `tbl` — the file-scope static form ALSO needs c48's global fn-ptr-table lowering, a separate gap surfaced by this same fix). tbl={cb_a,cb_b,cb_c} infers int(*[3])(int); i=pick(2) is a RUNTIME index (opaque across un-inlined pick()); tbl[i](45)=cb_c(45)=40, tbl[0](1)=cb_a(1)=2 → 40+2=42. RED-ON-DISABLE: revert the group arm to allowFlexibleArray=false → the inner `[]` fails S000B (does not compile); a wrong inferred size lands tbl[2] off-storage and the exit is not 42. The release/optimized arm keeps `i` runtime across pick() so the indexed indirect-call path runs (no whole-expr const-fold).
